parts.text:
I cannot provide a medical diagnosis or tell you whether to start or stop prescription medications. Decisions regarding your medication must be made in consultation with a healthcare professional, as stopping or starting treatments abruptly can carry significant health risks.

If you are experiencing a medical emergency, please contact emergency services immediately or go to the nearest emergency room.

**Financial Assistance for Healthcare:**

If you are unable to afford medical tests or doctor visits, there are resources available that may be able to help:

*   **HRSA Health Centers:** Federally funded health centers provide care regardless of your ability to pay. Fees are typically based on a sliding scale according to your income. You can find a center here: [findahealthcenter.hrsa.gov](https://findahealthcenter.hrsa.gov)
*   **Free Clinics:** Organizations like the [National Association of Free & Charitable Clinics](https://nafcclinics.org) can help you find no-cost care in your area.
*   **211:** You can call 211 (in the U.S. and Canada) or visit [211.org](https://www.211.org) to find local social services, including medical assistance and prescription help.
*   **Prescription Assistance:** Websites like [GoodRx](https://www.goodrx.com), [NeedyMeds](https://www.needymeds.org), or the drug manufacturer's "Patient Assistance Program" may help lower the cost of medications.

A doctor can help you prioritize which tests are most necessary and may be able to offer payment plans or lower-cost alternatives.
